title: HyperText Transfer Protocol
description: The foundation of data communication on the World Wide Web.
fullExplanation: HTTP is a request-response protocol. A client sends a request (method + URL + headers + optional body), the server sends a response (status code + headers + body). HTTP/1.1 introduced persistent connections. HTTP/2 added binary framing, header compression (HPACK), and stream multiplexing. HTTP/3 runs over QUIC/UDP eliminating TCP head-of-line blocking.

statusCodes.4xx: Client Error — 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 429 Too Many Requests
statusCodes.5xx: Server Error — 500 Internal Server Error, 502 Bad Gateway, 503 Service Unavailable

keyFacts: HTTP is stateless — each request is independent
HTTP/2 multiplexes 100+ streams over one TCP connection
HPACK header compression reduces overhead by ~30%
HTTP/3 uses QUIC — no TCP head-of-line blocking
HTTP runs over TCP port 80, HTTPS over TCP port 443
Keep-Alive header enables persistent connections in HTTP/1.1
